display_text: Does your AI system or use case fall within any of the exclusion categories listed in Article 2 of the EU AI Act? Select the option that best applies.
hint_text: Article 2 divides exclusions into 'hard' (assessment ends, no obligations) and 'soft' (partial, assessment continues to R3). Hard exclusions: military/national security, and third-country law enforcement. Soft exclusions: scientific research, open-source components under free licence, and purely personal non-professional use. For soft exclusions the system may still fall under Article 5 prohibited-practice rules, so the assessment must continue.

options.label: The AI system is used exclusively for military, national security, defence purposes, OR by a public authority in a third country for law enforcement and judicial cooperation with the EU
Scientific research and development — the system is developed and used solely for the purpose of scientific R&D (not yet placed on the market or in service)
Open-source AI component — the system is released under a free and open-source licence and is not yet part of a high-risk, prohibited, GPAI, or transparency-triggering deployment
Purely personal non-professional use — a natural person uses the AI system solely for personal, non-commercial activities
None of the above — no exclusion applies; the AI Act applies in full to this system
